title: erica arborea subsp. lusitanica
intro: Erica arborea subsp. lusitanica, also known as the Portuguese tree heath, is a subspecies of Erica arborea, a flowering plant native to the Iberian Peninsula. It is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to stimulate the immune system and reduce inflammation. The plant is used to treat various health issues, including respiratory problems, such as bronchitis and asthma, and skin conditions like eczema and acne, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. In herbal preparations, Erica arborea subsp. lusitanica is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are believed to promote overall well-being and provide relief from symptoms associated with various health conditions.
